QF.id: VIII Introduction to Clearing and Settlement System_74

question.text: Since February 1, 2025, SEBI mandates that trading members must:

answer.text: Collect the entire option premium upfront from options buyers before allowing the trade
Collect only 50% of the option premium upfront from buyers
Allow buyers to pay the premium on T+1 settlement basis
Waive premium collection if the buyer has sufficient margin balance

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI's measure effective Feb 1, 2025 requires full upfront collection of option premium from buyers. This limits undue leverage and mitigates risk of buyers taking positions without adequate funds. **The Trap:** This is a 2025 change. Earlier, some brokers allowed intraday positions without full premium. Now 100% upfront is mandatory. **Cross-Exam Context:** Recent SEBI investor protection measures are high-probability exam questions. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Full premium upfront from Feb 2025. No more intraday leverage on option buying without paying full premium.
